articles.title: Another COVID-19 death reported in A.P., three more test positive
articles.sum: The death of 66-year-old man has been attributed to comorbidities, and not the COVID-19 infection alone

articles.title: Kota C-section victims demand kidney transplant in 48 hours, write to President
articles.sum: The women have undergone 32 rounds of dialysis in the past 68 days

articles.title: Kerala’s government hospitals face a shortage of specialist doctors
articles.sum: Over 280 specialist doctor posts remain vacant in government hospitals, according to RTI data; more than 50 hospitals with delivery points lack adequate teams of gynaecologists, paediatricians and anaesthetists; doctors’ association calls for urgent recruitment, creation of more ...

articles.title: Bengaluru: Apartment residents seek builder accountability, parking norms in new Bill
articles.sum: One stakeholder called for the Bill to prescribe clear timelines for conducting association elections and define the tenure of office-bearers

articles.title: NPCIL denies nuclear systems breach after Kudankulam data leak report
articles.sum: India's Nuclear Power Corporation stated a data breach involves conventional plant facilities. The reported leaked files pertain only to non-nuclear safety systems. These documents were prepared by Reliance Infrastructure Ltd. for common services. The breach does not compromise n...

articles.title: 'Not in good shape but ...': Sonam Wangchuk refuses to end fast despite health concerns
articles.sum: Sonam Wangchuk continues his hunger strike for nineteen days, stating his health is not critical. He urged supporters to join a Parliament march on July 20 instead of asking him to break his fast. Wangchuk believes his protest sends a message about government accountability and t...

articles.title: Ficci chief: It's for industry to take advantage of FTAs
articles.sum: Economic momentum sustains as oil prices moderate and trade deals offer new opportunities. Corporate sector performance shows robust demand, with automotive and banking sectors leading growth. Manufacturing faces margin stress due to inventory, while IT remains under pressure. In...

articles.title: 35 years on, 103-yr-old woman wins legal battle for plot of land
articles.sum: Habiban Khatoon, aged 103, finally received justice after a 35-year legal battle. Her eldest son purchased land in her name, but a cousin took possession. She filed a title suit in 1994, facing appeals up to the High Court. After years of proceedings and delays, she regained poss...

articles.title: Patna high court ruling on groping draws criticism from top court
articles.sum: The Supreme Court expressed serious concern over a Patna High Court order. This order stated that removing a woman's salwar did not constitute attempted rape. The apex court emphasized judges must research and follow its previous rulings. It directed all courts to adhere to judic...

articles.title: Move high court on SIR-linked ration card cancellation: Supreme Court to petitioner
articles.sum: The Supreme Court declined to hear a petition regarding ration card cancellations. These cancellations followed voter list deletions due to doubtful citizenship status. The court directed the petitioner to pursue his appeal before the relevant tribunal. A favorable tribunal outco...

articles.title: Crackdown on 'objectionable' books in Indian-administered Kashmir sparks row
articles.sum: Authorities in Indian-administered Kashmir have asked educational institutions to review every book on their premises.

articles.title: Air India crash report likely to be ready in October, say investigators
articles.sum: The crash killed 260 people, including 241 on board, with only one passenger surviving.

articles.title: From Wimbledon towels to Scotch: What India-UK trade deal could mean for shoppers
articles.sum: The free trade agreement between the world's fifth and sixth largest economies has come into effect - can it deliver?

articles.title: How India's Lord's win echoes the legacy of its pioneering women cricketers
articles.sum: India won the first women's Test at Lord's, 142 years after its first men's Test.
